data.title: Hong Kong sees 37,806 drop in negative equity mortgages by mid-2025, signaling housing market recovery.
data.description: By June 2025, there were 37,806 cases of negative equity residential mortgage loans in Hong Kong, marking a decrease according to the Hong Kong Monetary Authority. This decrease suggests a slight improvement in the housing market conditions.

data.title: AI Security Challenged by Semantic Prompt Injections; Shift from Input Filtering to Output Defenses Needed. #AI #Cybersecurity
data.description: There have been new advancements in artificial intelligence that reveal weaknesses in multimodal models because of semantic prompt injections. This underscores the need to transition from filtering inputs to implementing defensive measures at the output level. This shift has become necessary to address vulnerabilities in these models and protect against potential threats. This development marks a crucial turning point in the field of AI, prompting researchers and developers to prioritize safeguarding outputs from adverse manipulation.

data.title: GitHub Actions launches new APIs for settings and moves 'windows-latest' to Windows Server 2025. (Read more)
data.description: GitHub Actions has introduced fresh REST APIs for handling settings, along with confirming the shift of the 'windows-latest' image tag to Windows Server 2025. This update brings enhanced functionality to users and ensures smoother operations on the platform.

data.title: Fed considers rate cut after EU tariff deal; less worried about inflation due to Trump's trade deals.
data.description: The Federal Reserve could be easing concerns about inflation prompted by tariffs following President Trump's recent string of trade agreements. This development may lead to the Fed considering a possible rate cut, especially after sealing a new tariff deal with the EU. This could potentially alter the Fed's outlook on economic policy moving forward.

data.title: Chainalysis reveals $530,000 in transactions linked to major CSAM site on the dark web since July 2022.
data.description: Chainalysis has uncovered a substantial cryptocurrency payment network connected to a significant Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) site on the dark web. The investigation has unveiled more than $530,000 worth of transactions occurring since July 2022.

data.title: SHARC Energy gives update on convertible debenture financing; not for U.S. distribution.
data.description: This press release should not be shared with US newswire services for distribution within the country.

data.title: Dow falls, Microsoft slips pre-earnings; Cathie Wood buys stock amid 47% drop. Elon Musk boosts Tesla. President Trump comments on tariffs.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline in the stock market while President Trump addressed the issue of tariffs. Tesla witnessed a surge in its stock after Elon Musk highlighted an AI deal. Additionally, notable investor Cathie Wood purchased shares of a struggling stock. In other news, Microsoft faced a drop in stock value ahead of its earnings report. Amid a 47% plunge, Cathie Wood invested heavily in this particular stock.

data.title: U.S.-EU trade deal boosts dollar, weakens euro as DXY jumps 1% in single-day surge. Euro hits its worst day since May.
data.description: A U.S.-EU trade deal has caused a surprising impact on currency markets, with the U.S. dollar surging by 1%, its best single-day performance in over two months, and the euro weakening significantly. The agreement involves a 15% tariff cap on EU goods, down from the previously threatened 30%, along with significant fiscal and energy concessions from the European side. The deal includes terms such as $750 billion in U.S. energy purchases over three years, a 150% increase from current levels. The SPY ETF hit record highs on July 28, with the U.S. dollar index (DXY) seeing its rare 1% daily rally after the trade agreement was announced.
First funding round of private placement by Giga Metals successfully completed.

data.title: T-Mobile US and T-Mobile USA announce final results of exchange offers for Array Digital Infrastructure's debt securities.
data.description: In Bellevue, Washington, T-Mobile US, Inc. (NASDAQ: TMUS) reported the completion of its Exchange Offers, in conjunction with its subsidiary T-Mobile USA, Inc., for certain series of senior notes from Array Digital Infrastructure, Inc. (formerly known as United States Cellular Corporation). The Exchange Offers have concluded, marking the final results of the initiative. This allowed for the exchange of any and all specified outstanding senior notes from Array, conducted under the Securities Exchange Act.

data.title: Energy stocks and the dollar rise after US-EU trade deal; S&P 500 hits record 6,400 points, Nasdaq 100 at 23,380 pts. Nvidia's stock hits $175 per share, $4.27 trillion market cap. US to impose 15% tariff on EU imports and EU pledges $750 billion for US energy exports.
data.description: The U.S. stock market saw a positive start to the week, with Wall Street slightly up, fueled by a surge in energy stocks, and the U.S. dollar showcasing its strongest performance in more than two months following the U.S.-EU trade deal announcement. The S&P 500 reached a new high of 6,400 points, gaining 0.1%, and the Nasdaq 100 surged 0.5% to break all-time records at 23,380 points. Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) experienced rapid movement in its stock price. NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) also hit a milestone, reaching $175 per share and boasting a colossal market capitalization of $4.27 trillion. Under the new trade agreement, the U.S. will implement a 15% tariff on EU imports, including pharmaceuticals and automobiles. Sectors like steel, aluminum, and copper will face even higher tariffs, potentially up to 50%. In exchange, the European Union committed to buying $750 billion in U.S. energy exports throughout the next three years, a significant increase from the previous yearly amount of around $100 billion. For further details, please refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dow remained steady post-Fed decision; Nvidia surged on target increase. Meta, Microsoft had strong earnings.Characters: 97
data.description: Following the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, the Dow Jones index remained unchanged. Nvidia saw a surge in stock value due to a raised price target. Today in the stock market, the Dow experienced a decrease following comments from Fed Chairman Powell. On the bright side, companies like Meta and Microsoft saw significant growth in their stock value after reporting impressive earnings.

data.title: Live Oak Bancshares, Inc. priced 4 million depositary shares at $25 each, representing a 1/40th ownership interest in Series A Preferred Stock.
data.description: On July 28, 2025, Live Oak Bancshares, Inc. disclosed the pricing details of their underwritten offering. The company priced 4,000,000 depositary shares at $25 each, where each one represents a 1/40th stake in a share of 8.375% Fixed Rate Series A Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock (known as the Series A Preferred Stock). This offering highlights the company's strategic financial moves and its positioning within the market for preferred stock.

data.title: Commissioner of BLS fired by Trump over alleged rigged jobs report, risking credibility of economic data.
data.description: Economic experts have raised concerns that removing the leader of the Bureau of Labor Statistics may erode faith in official economic statistics. This move has the potential to shake confidence in key government data that is vital for policy-making and decision-making processes. The Bureau of Labor Statistics plays a crucial role in gathering and disseminating essential economic information, influencing markets, businesses, and individuals. The sudden displacement of the head of this agency could have broader implications for the credibility and reliability of economic data, impacting various sectors and potentially leading to skepticism among the public. Maintaining the independence and integrity of statistical agencies is crucial to ensure transparency and trust in economic indicators. Efforts to safeguard the accuracy and impartiality of such data are essential for preserving the credibility of government economic information.

data.title: Celcuity Inc. plans $150M convertible notes offering and $75M common stock offering for targeted oncology therapies.
data.description: On July 28, 2025, Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC), a biotech company in Minneapolis, revealed plans for underwritten public offerings. The proposals include $150,000,000 aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 (Convertible Notes Offering) and $75,000,000 of common stock shares (Common Stock Offering). Celcuity focuses on developing targeted therapies for oncology, marking an important step in their clinical-stage journey.
THEON releases first half of 2025 Trading Update, showcasing strong performance and revenue growth.

data.title: Scott Bessent optimistic on US-China trade deal as Trump imposes tariffs; talks progress in Stockholm towards a potential agreement that benefits both nations.
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent expressed optimism on Friday about the prospect of reaching a trade agreement with China amidst President Donald Trump's initiation of new tariffs impacting numerous countries. Bessent shared on social media platform X (previously known as Twitter) that recent trade discussions in Stockholm have made significant headway towards a potential U.S.-China deal, as reported by Reuters. He indicated that progress has been made, stating, "This week's negotiations in Stockholm have advanced our talks with China, and I believe that we have the makings of a deal that will benefit both of our great nations." Despite mentioning that the deal is not finalized, Bessent conveyed hope for a positive outcome. The information was later reposted due to a technical glitch. The Treasury Secretary also mentioned during an interview with CNBC on Thursday that while the deal is not yet sealed, U.S. negotiators have been assertive in negotiations. For further details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Backpack Exchange partners with Sei Network to improve blockchain access with a fast Layer 1 solution, benefiting crypto users.
data.description: The Backpack Exchange platform has integrated the Sei Network to facilitate digital asset interactions on a rapid Layer 1 blockchain. This strategic decision is set to enhance the user experience and accessibility in the realm of cryptocurrency trading. The integration promises faster transaction speeds and improved efficiency for users engaging with the platform. This development signifies a step towards advancing the digital asset ecosystem, with implications for the seamless and efficient trading experience.
APPLE’S COOK TELLS EMPLOYEES THAT PRODUCT PIPELINE IS ‘AMAZING’

data.title: GitHub improves pull request experience with rich diffs for images and markdown, updated tree ordering, and better performance.
data.description: GitHub has introduced new features to the pull request 'Files changed' page, including rich image and markdown diffs, enhanced tree ordering, and improved performance. Users can now experience a more streamlined and visually appealing interface when reviewing changes. This update aims to provide a more efficient and user-friendly experience for developers on the platform.
